items.hook: Rickie, Melvin and Dean McCullough to leave Radio 1 in schedule shake-up
items.body: Jeremiah Asiamah will join Charlie Hedges in the Live Lounge, among other changes.

items.hook: Interview: Élie Wajeman • Director of Comète - “Paris is still vibrant, dangerous, moving, and violent” - /France
items.body: The French filmmaker recounts the journey of an ensemble film that began as an alternative experiment in filmmaking and genre blending

items.hook: Hidden Creatures by Dino Martins review – the revolting world of parasites
items.body:
From maggots to viruses, this gross-out compendium also manages to celebrate the awe and inventiveness of nature

items.hook: What we’re reading: writers and readers on the books they enjoyed in June
items.body:
Candice Carty-Williams, Patrick Freyne and Guardian readers discuss the titles they have read over the last month. Join the conversation in the comments

items.hook: WGA adds its own suit to Paramount-Warner merger opposition
items.body: The guild says the merger would create incentive and ability to suppress writers’ wages.

items.hook: Stanisław Stadnicki was a Polish nobleman and the Lord Starosta of Żygwulsko (Sigulda).
items.body: He was a known troublemaker, called 'the Devil of Łańcut' for his violent behaviour. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: She was President of the Latvian Chamber of Commerce from 2008 to 2011.
items.body: Žaneta Jaunzeme-Grende is a Latvian politician and businesswoman. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Aftermath of World War I: The Parliament of Poland establishes Silesian Voivodeship before the Polish-German plebiscite.
items.body: The aftermath of World War I saw far-reaching and wide-ranging cultural, economic, and social change across Europe, Asia, and Africa, and in areas outside those that were directly involved. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Refugiados ("Refugees") is a monument and instance of public art in Madrid, Spain.
items.body: Located at a water pond of the Paseo de Recoletos, it is dedicated to migrants and refugees. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Carmen Pujals was an Argentine botanist.
items.body: She was born in Buenos Aires, Argentina and moved to Barcelona at the age of five. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Great Powers of Europe conclude the convention of London in order to stabilise the Near East.
items.body: The European balance of power is a tenet in international relations that no single power should be allowed to achieve hegemony over a substantial part of Europe. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: He was the founding executive director of the Charter for Compassion, launched in 2008 by TED.com and Karen Armstrong, with the mission of supporting the emergence of a "global compassion movement".
items.body: Andrew Himes is an American writer and editor. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Counterknowledge: How We Surrendered to Conspiracy Theories, Quack Medicine, Bogus Science and Fake History is a polemic by British writer and journalist Damian Thompson which examines the dissemination and reception of fringe theories.
items.body: It was published on 1 January 2008 by Atlantic Books and is Thompson's third book. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The 321 series is a DC electric multiple unit (EMU) commuter train type operated by West Japan Railway Company (JR-West) in the Kansai Region of Japan.
items.body: One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The flock bronzewing, also known as the flock pigeon, harlequin bronzewing and the harlequin pigeon is a species of pigeon in the family Columbidae.
items.body: It is endemic to drier parts of Australia. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Interview: Mike Chang • Distributor, Andrews Film - “Our ambition is not only to distribute films in Taiwan, but to grow alongside our audience” - Distributor of the Month
items.body: The managing director of the Taiwanese outfit speaks about distribution as a form of curation, and about building collections of films that create an engaging thematic dialogue

items.hook: CEE Animation Forum announces its selection - CEE Animation 2026
items.body: A total of 33 projects have been selected for the 14th edition of the pitching, financing and co-production event for animation, taking place in Pilsen from 10 to 12 November

items.hook: The division was formed in 1985 as it replaced the League of Ireland B Division as the League of Ireland's second level division.
items.body: The League of Ireland First Division, also known as the SSE Airtricity League First Division for sponsorship reasons, is the second-highest division in both the League of Ireland and the Republic of Ireland football league system. Worth a quiet trip down the r

items.hook: Baruch Laibov and Alexander Voznitsyn are burned alive in St. Petersburg, Russia. Vonitzin had converted to Judaism with Laibov's help, with the consent of Empress Anna Ivanovna.
items.body: Baruch Leibov was a Jewish merchant who was burnt to death at the stake in St. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Eighteen Nobel laureates sign the Mainau Declaration against nuclear weapons, later co-signed by thirty-four others.
items.body: The Nobel Prizes are awards administered by the Nobel Foundation and granted in accordance with the principle of "for the greatest benefit to humankind". The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Space Shuttle program: Endeavour is launched on STS-127 to complete assembly of the International Space Station's Kibō module.
items.body: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The song was released by Immortal Records on July 23, 2002, as the lead single from the band's self-titled debut album.
items.body: "Capricorn (A Brand New Name)" is the debut song by American rock band Thirty Seconds to Mars. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The State of North Borneo, now Sabah, Malaysia, is annexed by the United Kingdom.
items.body: North Borneo was a British protectorate in the northern part of the island of Borneo. One of those things you can't un-know.
